source_priority: ISO OPM standard (ISO/PDPAS 19450, 2014 draft of ISO/PAS 19450)
Dov Dori founder methodology (Dori 2002, Object-Process Methodology)
OPM language/book material (Dori 2002 front-matter summary tables)

object.definition: Model element representing a thing that does or might exist physically or informatically; persistent existence; remains in its current implicit or explicit state until a process acts on it.
allowed_relations: source of: consumption, agent, instrument, condition(any incoming kind), event(any incoming kind), effect(input side)
structural with other objects: aggregation, exhibition, generalization, instantiation, tagged
exhibition with a process (object as attribute of a process, or process as operation of an object)
forbidden_relations: procedural link to another object (procedural links connect object<->process only, except process-process invocation/exception)
being 'affected' if stateless (stateless objects can only be created or consumed; ISO 3.3 NOTE)
validation_rules: OBJ-1: every object has a unique identifying name in the model (ISO 6.2.2 para 483-485)
OBJ-2: name is a noun phrase, bold, capitalized per word in OPL (ISO 7.1.2)
OBJ-3: generic properties Perseverance=static, Essence in {physical, informatical}, Affiliation in {systemic, environmental} (ISO 7.3.3)
OBJ-4: if stateful, the state set is explicit; at most one default state; 0+ initial, 0+ final states (ISO 7.3.5.3)
software_hints: LATER PHASE ONLY: candidate entity/class/resource; physical objects may map to monitored assets rather than data; informatical objects to data structures

process.definition: Transformation of one or more objects: generation, effect (state change), or consumption; has positive time duration; cannot exist in isolation from objects.
allowed_relations: target of: consumption, agent, instrument, condition, event links (from objects/states)
structural with other processes: aggregation, exhibition, generalization, instantiation, tagged
exhibition with objects (attributes of the process)
forbidden_relations: owning states (no process states; status is modelled by subprocesses; ISO 3.68 NOTE)
existing without at least one transforming link to an object or state (ISO 8.1.2 para 734)
validation_rules: PRC-1: passes the object-process test: time association, verb association, transforms at least one object (ISO 7.3.2)
PRC-2: non-empty preprocess object set and non-empty postprocess object set (ISO 8.2.2 para 763, 768)
PRC-3: at a given abstraction level, each linked object/state has exactly one procedural link and one role w.r.t. this process (ISO 8.1.2)
PRC-4: name preferably a gerund; bold, capitalized per word in OPL (Dori front matter; ISO B.6.3)
software_hints: LATER PHASE ONLY: candidate operation/service/workflow step with explicit pre/postconditions and duration; NOT automatically an API endpoint

state.definition: Possible situation or position of an object; meaningful only within its owning object; a value is a state of an attribute.
allowed_relations: source of: state-specified consumption, state-specified agent, state-specified instrument, condition state-specified links, state-specified event links, effect input link
target of: state-specified result, effect output link
value role when the owning object is an attribute
forbidden_relations: being source/target of fundamental structural relations on its own (aggregation/generalization/instantiation between states are not defined in the sources)
validation_rules: STA-1: every state belongs to exactly one object (ISO 7.3.5.1 para 668)
STA-2: label bold, lowercase in OPL (ISO 7.3.5.2 para 678)
STA-3: at most one default state per object; initial/final markers use correct notation (ISO 7.3.5.3-7.3.5.4)
STA-4: any state referenced by a link is a member of the owning object's permissible state set
software_hints: LATER PHASE ONLY: candidate status value / lifecycle stage with transition table derived ONLY from effect links; NOT automatically a database column

structural.name: aggregation-participation
structural.definition: Refineable (whole) aggregates one or more refinees (parts).
structural.graphical_notation: solid black triangle, apex to whole, base to parts; incompleteness bar variant
structural.opl_template: Whole consists of Part1, Part2, ..., and Partn.
validation_rules: whole and parts all objects or all processes (ISO 10.3.1 para 1542)
incomplete part collections must use the 'at least one other part' form (ISO para 1570)

structural.name: exhibition-characterization
structural.definition: Refineable (exhibitor) exhibits features (attributes = object features, operations = process features) that characterize it.
structural.meaning: thing-characteristic static relation; only structural relation allowed across the object-process boundary
structural.graphical_notation: small filled triangle inside larger empty triangle, apex to exhibitor
structural.opl_template: Exhibitor exhibits Attribute1, ..., and Attributen, as well as Operation1, ..., Operationm.
validation_rules: attribute is an object; operation is a process (ISO 10.3.3.1 para 1592)
object exhibitor lists attributes before operations; process exhibitor lists operations first (ISO para 1610)
attribute states are its values; instance values must fall in class value range (ISO 10.3.5.1 para 1750)
software_hints: fields/properties and methods of the owning concept; deferred

structural.name: generalization-specialization
structural.definition: Refineable (general) generalizes refinees (specializations), which inherit its parts, features, tagged structural links, and procedural links.
structural.meaning: is-a / inheritance static relation; multiple inheritance allowed
structural.opl_template: Specialization1, Specialization2, ..., and Specializationn are General. (single: Specialization is a General.)
validation_rules: general and specializations share Perseverance (ISO 10.3.4.1 para 1655)
inheritance covers parts, features, tagged structural links, procedural links (ISO 10.3.4.2 para 1683-1688)
discriminating attribute values constrain specializations (ISO 10.3.4.3)

structural.name: classification-instantiation
structural.definition: Refineable (class) classifies refinees (instances) created by providing explicit values for the class pattern.
structural.graphical_notation: small filled circle inside empty triangle, apex to class
structural.opl_template: Instance is an instance of Class. (plural: Instance1, ..., and Instancen are instances of Class.)
validation_rules: instance attribute values within class value specification (ISO 10.3.5.1 para 1750-1752)
no complete/incomplete collection distinction (ISO NOTE 3 para 1764)
instantiation does not form hierarchies (Dori front matter; assumption w.r.t. ISO)

structural.name: tagged structural link (unidirectional|null|bidirectional|reciprocal)
structural.definition: User-defined static relation between two objects or two processes, with modeller-supplied tag text.
structural.meaning: any static association not covered by the four fundamental relations
structural.graphical_notation: open-arrowhead arrow (uni); harpoon heads both ends (bi); tag annotations
structural.opl_template: Source tag Destination. | Source relates to Destination. | A and B are reciprocity-tag. | A and B are related.
validation_rules: never object<->process (ISO 10.1 para 1467-1469)
bidirectional tagged emits one sentence per direction (ISO 10.2.3 para 1499)

procedural.name: consumption link
procedural.definition: Transforming link: the process consumes (destroys, eliminates) the consumee.
procedural.meaning: consumee existence (possibly at state) is precondition; object eliminated on activation; preprocess set only
procedural.graphical_notation: closed-arrowhead arrow from consumee (or its state) to process
procedural.opl_template: Processing consumes Consumee. | Process consumes specified-state Object.
validation_rules: wait semantics unless c/e modifier
object cannot also be resultee of the same process (fact consistency ISO 14.2.3)
optional rate property + quantity attribute for gradual consumption (ISO 9.1.2 para 818-821)

procedural.name: result link
procedural.definition: Transforming link: the process creates (generates, yields) the resultee.
procedural.meaning: resultee creation is postcondition on completion; postprocess set only
procedural.graphical_notation: closed-arrowhead arrow from process to resultee (or its state)
procedural.opl_template: Processing yields Resultee. | Process yields specified-state Object.
validation_rules: no event/condition control modifier on result links (ISO 8.2.3 NOTE 1)
if resultee has an initial state, attach the link to the object, not the initial state (ISO 9.3.2 para 954)

procedural.name: effect link
procedural.definition: Transforming link: the process affects the affectee, changing its state (unspecified or state-specified).
procedural.source_type: object or its input state (input side); process (output side)
procedural.target_type: process (input side); object or its output state (output side)
procedural.meaning: state change; affectee in pre- and postprocess sets; in transition during performance
procedural.graphical_notation: bidirectional double-arrowhead arrow; state-specified variants split into input and output links
procedural.opl_template: Processing affects Affectee. | Process changes Object from s1 to s2. | Process changes Object from s1. | Process changes Object to s2.
validation_rules: affectee must be stateful (ISO 3.3 NOTE; 3.15 NOTE)
input-specified form requires default state or state probability distribution (ISO 9.3.3.3)
'affects' is a legal abstraction of 'changes from/to' (ISO 14.2.3 EXAMPLE)

procedural.name: agent link
procedural.definition: Enabling link from a human (or human group) enabler to the process it enables and controls.
procedural.source_type: object in agent role | its state (state-specified)
procedural.meaning: necessary for activation and throughout performance; not transformed; disappearance aborts the process
procedural.graphical_notation: line ending in filled circle (black lollipop) at process
procedural.opl_template: Agent handles Processing. | Specified-state Agent handles Processing.
validation_rules: agent is human/group (ISO 3.4); organizations admissible per Dori 5.2.4
agent unchanged by the process, else remodel as transformee (Dori 5.2.4)
enabler present throughout performance (ISO 9.2.1 para 862)

procedural.name: instrument link
procedural.definition: Enabling link from a non-human enabler required for the process to occur.
procedural.source_type: object in instrument role | its state (state-specified)
procedural.meaning: required, neither consumed nor changed; disappearance aborts the process
procedural.graphical_notation: line ending in empty circle (white lollipop) at process
procedural.opl_template: Processing requires Instrument. | Processing requires specified-state Instrument.
validation_rules: instrument non-human (ISO 3.30)
unchanged by the process; if changed, remodel as affectee (Dori 5.2.5)
present throughout performance (ISO 9.2.1)

procedural.name: condition link (family: condition consumption|effect|agent|instrument, plain or state-specified)
procedural.definition: Control link: an incoming transforming/enabling link annotated 'c', adding skip semantics - if the source object (at the specified state, if any) does not exist, the process is bypassed rather than waited for.
procedural.meaning: guard with bypass; skip semantics overrides wait semantics of co-occurring non-condition links
procedural.graphical_notation: underlying link symbol with letter 'c' near the process end
procedural.opl_template: Process occurs if Object exists|is state, in which case <consumption/effect clause>, otherwise Process is skipped. / Agent handles Process if Agent exists|is state, else Process is skipped.
validation_rules: no condition modifier on outgoing (result) links (ISO 8.2.3 NOTE 1)
referenced state must be a permissible state of the source object
skip overrides wait when both present (ISO 8.2.3 para 782-787)
disambiguate from state-specified (wait) links during import (conflict C-1)
software_hints: if/else guard, bypass branch in workflow; deferred

procedural.name: event link (family: consumption|effect|agent|instrument event, plain or state-specified)
procedural.definition: Control link: an incoming link annotated 'e'; creation/appearance/state-entry of the source object initiates precondition evaluation of the process; the event is then lost.
procedural.meaning: initiator; couples triggering with the underlying transforming/enabling semantics
procedural.graphical_notation: underlying link symbol with letter 'e' near the process end/arrowhead
procedural.opl_template: Object initiates Process, which consumes|affects|requires Object. / Agent initiates and handles Process.
validation_rules: no result event link (ISO 8.2.3 NOTE 1)
event lost after evaluation regardless of outcome (ISO 9.5.1 para 1089)

procedural.name: invocation link (incl. self-invocation)
procedural.definition: Control link between two processes: completion of the source immediately initiates the destination; implies a transient object created and immediately consumed.
procedural.graphical_notation: jagged lightning arrow with closed arrowhead at invoked process
procedural.opl_template: Invoking-process invokes invoked-process. | Invoking-process invokes itself.
validation_rules: both ends are processes
invocation occurs only on successful completion (ISO 9.5.2.5.1 NOTE 1)

procedural.name: overtime|undertime exception link
procedural.definition: Control link from a process violating its duration bounds to an exception-handling process.
procedural.graphical_notation: link with one (overtime) or two (undertime) short oblique bars near the handler
procedural.opl_template: Overtime Handling Process occurs if duration of Source Process exceeds max-duration time-units. | Undertime Handling Process occurs if duration of Source Process falls short of min-duration time-units.
validation_rules: source process must declare the relevant duration bound (ISO 9.5.4.1)

opl_templates_note: Full template inventory with examples and statuses is in 05_opl_grammar_templates.md (32 procedural + 15 structural + 6 thing/state templates, all ISO-confirmed unless marked).

validation_rules.description: Every model element is a thing (object|process), a state owned by an object, or a link; nothing else.
Every link is classified structural or procedural; control links are procedural.
Structural links connect like-kind things only, except exhibition-characterization.
Procedural links connect an object/state with a process; only invocation/exception connect two processes.
Procedural link uniqueness: one procedural link and one role per object(or state)-process pair at a given abstraction.
Every process connects via a transforming link to >=1 object or state; pre- and postprocess sets non-empty.
Affectees must be stateful; stateless objects only created/consumed.
States referenced by any state-specified link belong to the linked object's permissible state set.
No result event or result condition links (no control modifiers on outgoing links).
Result link should not target an initial state of the resultee.
Aggregation/generalization preserve Perseverance between refineable and refinees.
Every OPM construct (link + endpoints) has exactly one corresponding OPL sentence; OPD and OPL are equivalent.
Fact consistency: no OPD may contradict a model fact of any other OPD in the model; refinement is not contradiction.
At most one default state per object; instance attribute values within class value ranges.
Input-specified effect links require a default state or state probability distribution on the affectee.
Same-kind links at one process not sharing a point = AND; sharing a common point = XOR/OR fan; fan semantics must be declared.
When importing informal 'occurs if in state' requirements, modeller intent (wait vs skip) must be resolved; default to state-specified (wait) and log an assumption.
validation_rules.source_reference: ISO 8.2.3 vs Dori 5.3.3 (conflict C-1); the default choice is this KB's convention, not a source rule

validation_rules.description: Abstraction link conflicts resolved by the semantic strength order of ISO 14.2.4.1.4.
Object-process test applied to every thing name whose kind is doubtful; nouns default to objects.
No software artifact generation before all error-severity checks pass and all assumptions are logged.
validation_rules.source_reference: Phase-1 mandate (user constraint); consistent with ISO 6.2.6 conceptual/runtime separation
